decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. Previous attempts to maximize spreader width and conductivity hit upper bounds. The strategy shifts to increasing the base plate conductivity, which acts as the downstream heat extraction path. Increasing base conductivity from 12.0 to 24.0 W/(m*K) (within the 2.0x step limit) will reduce thermal resistance between the spreader/chip assembly and the cooled boundaries.

changes.reason: Increase base plate conductivity to improve heat conduction from the high-conductivity spreader to the Dirichlet boundaries at x=0 and x=1.2, addressing the bottleneck after spreader optimization stalled.

expected_effects: Reduction in overall thermal resistance of the assembly
Lower peak temperature in the chip component
Move feasibility status from violated to satisfied or closer to the 85.0°C limit
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the interface resistance between base and spreader becomes dominant
Further increases in base conductivity beyond this step may be required if the margin remains negative
